Notes: Dynamic gate biasing on the RX circuit. DMOS 180nm BCD-SOI devices being driven by external 0.35µm HV CMOS gate driver / level shifters.
Very interesting technique of averaging both places by short-circuiting them for achieving 3 voltage levels and reduce power dissipation per channel.
Interesting use of Off-chip DC-DC-Conv for reference generation. Very interesting use of vegetable oil for approximating the acoustic attenuation of the media to that of soft-tissue.
Uses Off-chip capacitors with on-chip HV switches for 5V supply multiplying through DC-DC converter implementation. Very interesting!

Interesting use of the Inductor to implement an LC tank that instead of using a charge-replenish middle-phase in a 3-level voltage pulser to reduce dynamic power dissipation it stores the dynamic energy in the inductor to use in the next cycle and driving the dynamic power dissipation related to charging the transducers lossy parastic branch to zero overtime (long num of cycles in  the PRF).
Shares the inductor based LC tank between multiple channels enabling the use of a single inductor for driving dynamic power dissipation towards zero.
On-chip switches with off-chip capacitors for implementing the DC-DC converters generating the 6 voltage levels excluding the ground voltage.
